steps.title: Identify the research finding in Text 2
steps.content:
Text 1 shows people hiding their preferences, assuming it makes them appear likable.

Text 2 directly refutes this: research proves that not stating preferences doesn't make you more likable—it actually makes decisions harder for others and makes them less happy.

The key signal is what Text 2 proves actually works.

steps.title: Apply this to the lunch scenario
steps.content: In the lunch scenario, the author of Text 2 would recommend doing what the research proves works: stating your preference. The remaining options either sidestep the decision or involve listening rather than expressing your own view. That matches choice C — C is correct.
